concerns.text: The plan targets GitHub Actions free-tier (2 CPU, 7 GB RAM) for processing OpenNeuro ds003645. This dataset contains high-density EEG (128 channels) from ~20 participants. Downloading the full BIDS dataset (~10-15 GB) and loading it into MNE-Python for ICA decomposition on a 7 GB RAM limit is infeasible; the process will OOM (Out Of Memory) before ICA can run. The plan's 'streaming' strategy is insufficient for ICA, which requires loading the entire continuous data matrix into RAM. A CPU-only, 7 GB RAM environment cannot support the proposed analysis of this specific dataset size.

The plan acknowledges ds003645 is not in the 'Verified datasets' list but proceeds assuming accessibility. While the upstream resolver guarantees URL reachability, the plan fails to verify if the dataset's *modality* and *size* match the compute constraints. ds003645 is a high-density EEG dataset (128 channels) which is significantly larger than typical 32-channel oddball datasets. The plan does not propose a subsampling strategy (e.g., selecting a subset of channels or participants) to fit the 7 GB RAM constraint, making the proposed analysis methodologically impossible on the target hardware.
